phases.description: Period where root causes accumulated: Lack of MOC process allowed outdated procedures to persist, and missing torque/tension verification standards were not enforced.
events: T-90d: Maintenance procedures for mechanical splices remained outdated, lacking OEM torque and tension verification checklists (5-Why Root Cause: No MOC process).
T-14d: Mechanical splice installation performed on Conveyor CV-03 without documented torque verification or tension monitoring (Fishbone Measurements: Missing records; Fault Tree BE-01/BE-02).

phases.description: Period following installation where misalignment and environmental factors stressed the weak splice, leading to progressive degradation.
events: T-30d: Pulley/Frame misalignment and sticky material buildup began exerting lateral forces on the belt, exacerbating stress on the improperly installed splice (Fishbone Machine/Environment: BE-04/BE-05).

T-1d: Increased throughput in May placed higher dynamic loads on the conveyor, accelerating fatigue in the splice area lacking proper tension verification (Data Collector Follow-up: Impact of increased throughput).

phases.description: The immediate sequence leading to the mechanical failure and trip.
events: T-5m: Belt tracking deviation exceeded safe limits due to combined misalignment and splice weakness, causing visible belt wander (Problem Statement: Belt misalignment trip).
T-0m: Longitudinal mechanical splice failure occurred, resulting in immediate loss of belt integrity and activation of the misalignment safety trip (Problem Statement: Splice failure and trip).

phases.description: Immediate post-failure response, isolation, and initial assessment.
events: T+0m: Conveyor CV-03 automatically shutdown via trip, halting primary crushing circuit operations and preventing further material spill (Effect: Immediate shutdown).
T+7.5h: Maintenance team completes initial isolation and inspection, confirming splice failure and initiating RCA process (Effect: 7.5 hours downtime).

incidentOverview.severity: Major - Production loss of 6,750 tons, $84,000 financial impact

preIncidentPeriod.description: Systemic degradation period characterized by lack of Management of Change (MOC) and improper installation practices.

events.event: Maintenance procedures for mechanical splices identified as outdated; OEM torque/tension guidelines not integrated.
events.notes: Root cause: No formal MOC process to trigger updates after previous incidents or design changes.

events.event: Mechanical splice installed on Conveyor CV-03 without torque verification records or tension monitoring.
events.source: Data Collector Gaps / Fault Tree (BE-01, BE-02)
events.notes: Installation performed by technicians lacking specific OEM certification (Fishbone Manpower). No quantitative tension data recorded.

events.event: Progressive stress accumulation due to pulley/frame misalignment and sticky material buildup.
events.notes: Structural misalignment and environmental debris exerted lateral forces on the weak splice, accelerating fatigue.

events.event: Increased throughput targets placed higher dynamic loads on conveyor system.
events.notes: Higher loads exacerbated the weakness in the improperly installed splice.

incidentPeriod.description: The immediate sequence of mechanical failure and protective trip.

events.event: Belt tracking deviation exceeded safe limits, causing visible belt wander.
events.notes: Misalignment trip condition initiated. Likely audible/visual warning before full failure.
Catastrophic loss of belt integrity at the splice joint.

events.event: Conveyor CV-03 automatic shutdown via misalignment safety trip.
events.notes: Emergency stop executed to prevent further damage and material spill.

postIncidentPeriod.description: Post-trip response, isolation, and initial assessment.

events.event: Primary crushing circuit operations halted; production loss begins.
Maintenance team isolates conveyor and begins initial inspection.
events.notes: Confirmation of splice failure and assessment of belt damage.

events.event: Initial RCA data collection initiated; downtime totals 7.5 hours.
events.notes: Financial impact calculated at $84,000. RCA pipeline triggered.

causalChain.cause: Absence of Management of Change (MOC) process
causalChain.effect: Maintenance procedures remained outdated, excluding OEM torque/tension specs

causalChain.cause: Outdated procedures + Lack of technician training
causalChain.effect: Splice installed without torque verification or tension monitoring

causalChain.cause: Weak splice integrity + Pulley/Frame misalignment + Sticky buildup
causalChain.effect: Progressive stress and fatigue on splice joint under increased load
Activation of misalignment trip and subsequent splice failure

simultaneousEvents.correlation: Rapid cascade: Misalignment stress caused splice failure, which triggered the safety trip.

responseTimeAnalysis.assessment: Safety systems functioned correctly by tripping the conveyor. Downtime duration reflects repair complexity due to lack of spare parts/pre-planning for splice failure.

earliestWarningSign.missedOpportunity: No hold point or supervisor verification required due to lack of procedure.

deviationProgression.notes: Misalignment and sticky buildup increasing lateral stress.

timelineGaps.description: Exact date of last procedure update or incident triggering need for update is unknown.
timelineGaps.impact: Cannot determine duration of systemic exposure.
timelineGaps.recommendation: Audit document control history for CV-03 procedures.

timelineGaps.description: No torque or tension records exist for the failed splice.
timelineGaps.recommendation: Mandate digital logging of torque/tension for future splices.

timelineGaps.description: No historical alignment survey data or tracking sensor trends.

immediateActions: Implement formal Management of Change (MOC) process for all maintenance procedures.
Update CV-03 splice installation procedure to include mandatory OEM torque and tension verification checklists.
Perform laser alignment survey of CV-03 pulleys and frame to correct misalignment.
shortTermActions: Retrain maintenance technicians on updated splice installation procedures and documentation requirements.
Procure and calibrate digital torque wrenches and belt tension meters.
Audit all recent mechanical splice installations on site for missing records.
longTermActions: Integrate mandatory digital logging (photos/data) of torque and tension into the CMMS for all critical splices.
Install permanent automated belt tracking and tension monitoring systems on critical conveyors.
Conduct full FMEA/PHA review for all conveyor systems to identify other critical control points lacking verification.

standardsReferenced: IS/ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1 - Control of production and service provision
IS/ISO 9001:2015 Clause 10.2 - Nonconformity and corrective action
ASTM E2659-16 - Standard Guide for Root Cause Analysis
CEMA Belt Conveyors for Bulk Materials - Splicing Guidelines
IEC 61508 - Functional safety of electrical/electronic/programmable electronic safety-related systems
